commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4463f3f1f66e31386fe4684747001d608350647a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Control UI sidebar All agents toggle that loads Recent Sessions without an agent scope and renders subagent/spawned rows in that mode, plus CSS and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +80. Total +148 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main scopes UI sessions.list requests by agent and the sidebar also filters out subagent/spawned rows. I did not run a live browser/gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this external PR has missing real behavior proof, product-direction overlap with sibling PRs, and code defects that affect session-state boundaries.

target.title: fix #95295: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95295
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Web UI sidebar Recent Sessions list could hide existing child-spawned subagent transcripts when the current chat scope was another agent, so users could not intentionally rediscover those sessions from the left sidebar.
- Solution: Keep the existing scoped/default behavior, but add an explicit sidebar **All agents** mode that uses the existing unscoped `sessions.list`/combined-store path and allows subagent/spawned rows in that opt-in view.
- What changed: Added sidebar state and UI for the scope toggle, dropped scoped `agentId` from `sessions.list` while that mode is active, kept cron/global/unknown/archived rows excluded, and added controller/rendering regression tests.
- What did NOT change: This does not change the chat picker, default sidebar scoping, gateway protocol schema, stored transcript format, migration behavior, or default visibility of subagent/spawned sessions.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users with persisted child-spawned subagent work can intentionally find and reopen those existing transcripts from the sidebar without losing the safer scoped default view.
- Fixes #95295

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sidebar Recent Sessions can now opt in to cross-agent discovery so an existing row such as `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` with `spawnedBy` can appear when the user enables **All agents**, while default scoped mode still hides those rows.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Windows/Git Bash with Node v22.17.0 and pnpm-installed workspace dependencies. The repo engine warning requested Node `>=22.19.0`, but the focused Vitest command completed successfully.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`; `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`; checked `command -v sonarlint || command -v sonar-scanner || command -v sonar-scanner-cli`.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused Vitest command exited 0 and covered the sidebar renderer plus session controller path. The diff whitespace check exited 0. The scanner availability command exited 1 because no SonarLint/sonar-scanner executable was available in PATH.
- **Observed result after fix:** The new controller regression verifies active sidebar all-agents mode sends `sessions.list` without `agentId`; the new renderer regression verifies a cross-agent spawned subagent row is displayed ahead of the main row and a cron row remains excluded.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a browser/manual UI session or a live gateway process with real persisted transcripts, and I could not run SonarLint because no scanner executable was available in PATH.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** N/A for new issue-mode PR; no existing review findings checklist applies.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, with neighboring focused suites `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` and `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` run for sidebar/chat rendering coverage.
- **Scenario locked in:** When sidebar all-agents mode is active, scoped `agentId` is omitted from `sessions.list`, and a cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` row with `spawnedBy` renders in Recent Sessions while cron rows remain filtered.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The controller test guards the request boundary that previously scoped away cross-agent rows, and the render test guards the sidebar filter that previously discarded subagent/spawned rows; together they cover the two root filters without expanding into unrelated gateway/protocol tests.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state / security-boundary / compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches session-state UI filtering and a scope control, which can affect whether internal child-spawned transcripts are visible. Compatibility risk is intentionally limited because no public protocol, schema, config, storage format, or migration contract changes. Related open PR scan found broader approaches for this issue, including PRs that added protocol/API surface; this PR intentionally chooses a narrower same-contract UI path instead.
- **Why acceptable:** The new behavior is default-off, explicitly user-selected, and limited to the sidebar Recent Sessions list. Existing exclusions for archived/global/unknown/cron rows remain. Default scoped behavior and chat picker behavior are not changed, so the security-boundary/session-state risk is bounded and reviewable.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root cause was duplicated scoping at the sidebar boundary: the refresh path requested `sessions.list` with the current agent's `agentId`, and the sidebar Recent Sessions filter then rejected subagent-keyed or `spawnedBy` rows. Those source filters prevented already-persisted child-spawned sessions from reaching the sidebar view across agent scopes.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix changes the two owner-side filters instead of masking missing rows downstream: opt-in all-agents mode stops sending the scoped `agentId` so the existing combined session store can return cross-agent rows, and the sidebar filter admits subagent/spawned rows only in that explicit mode while preserving cron/global/unknown/archive guards.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings for added `return false` guard branches are expected filter predicates, not symptom-masking defaults: they preserve existing exclusions for cron/global/unknown/archived sessions and the default-off subagent/spawned hiding behavior. The public-contract warning is addressed by keeping the canonical protocol/schema unchanged and using the existing unscoped `sessions.list` behavior.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary remains the existing `sessions.list` contract and combined session store; this patch does not add schema/protocol/config/data-format changes. The canonical behavior change is confined to the Web UI sidebar state/render/request boundary, with related open PRs considered and broader protocol changes left out of scope.
